Every day your field team submits data, analysts spend hours spot-checking spreadsheets — and still miss 30–40% of quality issues. There's a better way.

Records checked by
Servallab — every single row
01 · The Problem

Manual QC
doesn't scale.

Traditional CATI quality control relies on analysts reviewing exports in Excel — a process that is slow, inconsistent, and fundamentally unable to keep pace with modern survey volumes.

Day 1–2

Data arrives from the field

Raw CSV downloaded from CATI system. Analyst begins manual review in Excel — filtering, sorting, spot-checking ~10–20% of records.

Day 3–4

Inconsistencies discovered — late

Interviewer fabrication, logic errors, and duration anomalies are found — but only after several interviews have already been compiled into analysis. Remediation is costly.

Day 5+

Report delivered — with undetected issues

~35% of quality issues remain undetected. Straightlining, subtle fabrication patterns, and verbatim gibberish pass through. Client receives compromised data.

With Servallab

QC complete in under a minute

Upload → configure → click Run QC. Every record checked. Interviewer risk scores computed. AI narrative generated. Excel report ready for sign-off.

02 · Head-to-Head

Manual vs
Automated QC

Eight dimensions where Servallab systematically outperforms traditional manual review.

Dimension ❌ Manual QC ✅ Servallab
Processing speed 6–8 hours / 1,000 interviews < 30 seconds
Data coverage 10–20% spot check only 100% of every record
Consistency Varies by reviewer; fatigue degrades quality 100% rule-consistent, every run
Issue detection rate ~60% (human eye misses patterns) 95%+ of defined rule violations
Interviewer analysis Ad-hoc, anecdotal, no scores Weighted risk score 0–100 per interviewer
Verbatim quality Qualitative spot-check by one analyst AI-scored on grammar, coherence, relevance
Cost per 1,000 records $200–500 (analyst labour) < $0.10 (compute + API)
Reproducibility Results differ between reviewers Identical results on every re-run
03 · By the Numbers

The numbers
don't lie.

Direct performance comparison across the metrics that matter most in CATI survey QC.

Time to QC 1,000 interviews
Manual — 8 hours (480 min)
Servallab — <1 min
Data coverage
Manual — 15%
Servallab — 100%
Issue detection rate
Manual — ~60%
Servallab — 95%+
Consistency (same rules applied every run)
Manual — ~72% (reviewer variance)
Servallab — 100%
04 · What Servallab Checks

10 checks.
Zero missed patterns.

Servallab runs a full pipeline of automated checks the moment you click Run QC — covering every known CATI data quality failure mode.

📊

Missing Value Analysis

Flags columns and rows with excessive missingness above your configured threshold.

Core
🔁

Duplicate Detection

Identifies exact and near-duplicate records using configurable subset columns.

Core
⏱️

Duration Check

Flags interviews completed too fast or too slow against your min/max thresholds.

Core
➡️

Straightlining Detection

Detects respondents who selected the same answer across all Likert-scale items.

Core
🧠

Logic Checks

Validates IF/THEN conditional rules — e.g. under-18 respondents must not have salary data.

Core
🕵️

Fabrication Detection

Identifies sequential ID patterns, zero-variance responses, and other fabrication signals.

Core
👤

Interviewer Risk Scoring

Computes a weighted 0–100 risk score per interviewer combining all flag dimensions.

Core
✍️

Verbatim Quality Scoring

AI scores open-text responses on grammar, coherence, relevance, and length quality.

AI · Groq
📝

AI Feedback Letters

Generates structured, professional feedback letters for flagged interviewers automatically.

AI · Groq
📋

AI Executive Summary

Produces a plain-English QC narrative and per-check explanations for non-technical stakeholders.

AI · Groq
05 · Return on Investment

The ROI case
is overwhelming.

For a mid-size CATI operation running 10 projects per month at 1,000 interviews each, the time and cost savings are immediate and compound over every wave.

800h
Hours saved
Per month vs manual QC (10 projects × 80h each)
$40K+
Labour cost saved
Monthly at $50/hr analyst rate per 10 projects
35%
More issues found
Issues caught vs manual review (100% vs 60% detection)
1 day
Faster sign-off
QC report generated the same day as data delivery
0
Reviewer variance
Same rules, same results — every analyst, every run
∞
Scale factor
100 interviews or 100,000 — same 30-second QC time

Methodology note: Time estimates based on ESOMAR benchmarks for CATI QC analyst effort (2023). Detection rates derived from comparative studies of automated vs human review in structured survey datasets. Cost estimates assume $50/hr blended analyst rate. Your figures may vary.

06 · How It Works

Upload → Configure
→ Done.

Servallab's pipeline is four steps. The entire process — from raw CSV to signed-off report — takes under two minutes.

1️⃣

Upload your data

Drag-and-drop CSV or Excel. Servallab previews columns, row count, and data types immediately.

2️⃣

Configure checks

Toggle checks on/off. Set thresholds, columns, and logic rules in the sidebar — no code required.

3️⃣

Run QC

Click Run QC. The full pipeline runs in the background — load, clean, validate, score — in seconds.

4️⃣

Review & export

Browse flagged rows by check, generate AI explanations, and download a full Excel report.

🔬

Ready to stop
doing QC manually?

Servallab is free to try. Upload your next wave of data and see every quality issue — in under a minute. No setup. No code. No surprises.

Open Servallab →
CSV · Excel · SAV supported  ·  No data stored beyond 1 hour  ·  AI features require free Groq API key
